query: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status latest news June July 2026, algae paint peeling, is the pool fully repaired, lawsuit status
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Is the pool fully repaired? No.** As of July 3, 2026, the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is **not fully repaired** and remains closed to the public.

**Current status:**
- Pool is fenced off with National Guard present; water is greenish-brown with returned algae
- Blue polyurea coating (Rhino Linings Pipeliner 5000) has been peeling/detaching since mid-June 2026
- Trump announced the pool will be **drained again for "permanent repair" around/after July 4, 2026**
- Rhino Linings' VP said repairs will take "weeks" and won't be done before Independence Day

**Cost:** Ballooned from an initial ~$1–2M estimate to nearly **$15–16 million**, plus a separate $1.7M no-bid nanobubbler contract to a Trump-donor-owned company.

**Paint peeling causes:** Experts cite multifactorial failure — poor granite surface prep, rushed 8-week timeline, incompatible substrate, heat/UV stress, and hydrogen peroxide — not vandalism.

**Vandalism claims:** Trump alleges sabotage and cites six to seven arrests; PBS and experts cast doubt.

**Lawsuit:** The Cultural Landscape Foundation (and CEO Charles Birnbaum), via the Washington Litigation Group, sued the Interior Department in U.S. District Court for D.C. (Case 26-cv-1593) to halt the blue painting, alleging violations of federal historic-preservation review laws. Congressional Democrats are also demanding investigations into the no-bid contracts.

Note: AskNews tools were unavailable (quota/tier limits) and several outlets blocked direct fetching, but I cross-validated findings across CNN, ABC News, Scientific American, Christian Science Monitor, CBS News, POLITICO/E&E, NPR, and the litigating firm's own release.

sources.title: A new plan to keep the Reflecting Pool clean – including annual purge of 'nasty' muck
sources.snippet: DOI soliciting bids for long-term maintenance contractor after months of trying to turn the memorial blue; new intensive cleaning plan including annual purge.

sources.snippet: Trump said the pool will be drained again for 'permanent repair' around July 4 and that six people have been arrested for alleged damage.

sources.snippet: Pool closed, greenish-brown water, chain-link fence and National Guard; costs exceeded $16M; Trump alleges sabotage; seven arrests; draining again after July 4.

sources.title: Why the paint is peeling off the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool—experts explain
sources.snippet: Coating is polyurea (Rhino Linings Pipeliner 5000) applied by AIC over granite; multifactorial failure causes; final cost ~$15M; repairs need draining, warranty-covered.

sources.snippet: Rhino Linings VP Francois Rivard said the peeling/cracked liner won't be fixed before July 4 celebrations; repairs will take weeks.

sources.snippet: Sika Corp. balked at the July 4 deadline and blue-bottom requirement months before blue strips began floating to the surface.

sources.snippet: TCLF lawsuit (Case 26-cv-1593, DDC) against Department of the Interior filed April 12.

sources.snippet: Experts cast doubt on Trump's vandalism/sabotage claims; pool about to be drained again.

sources.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
sources.snippet: In 2026 the pool was resurfaced at Trump's order with a blue polyurea coating and a nanobubbler filtration system.

key_facts: As of July 3, 2026, the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is NOT fully repaired: it remains closed/fenced with National Guard, algae has returned, and the blue coating is peeling.
Trump stated (~July 1, 2026) the pool will be drained AGAIN for a 'permanent repair' around/after July 4, 2026.
Rhino Linings VP Francois Rivard told POLITICO the coating repairs will take 'weeks' and 'will not be before' the July 4 celebrations.
The coating is polyurea (Rhino Linings Pipeliner 5000) applied over granite by Atlantic Industrial Coatings (AIC); it began detaching/peeling within weeks (peeling reported June 16-18, 2026).
Final project cost is reported as nearly $15-16 million, versus an initial estimate of roughly $1-2 million (Trump said ~$1M).
A separate $1.7 million no-bid contract for a nanobubbler water-cleaning system went to a company owned by a Trump donor.
Experts attribute the coating failure to multiple factors (poor granite surface prep, rushed 8-week timeline, incompatible substrate, heat/UV, hydrogen peroxide) rather than to vandalism.
Trump alleged sabotage/vandalism and cited six (later seven) arrests; PBS and experts cast doubt on the sabotage claims.

Lawsuit: The Cultural Landscape Foundation and CEO Charles Birnbaum (via Washington Litigation Group) sued the Interior Department in U.S. District Court for D.C. (Case 26-cv-1593) to halt the blue painting, alleging violations of federal historic-preservation review/public-input laws.
Algae returned within days of refilling; the darker blue floor absorbs more heat, accelerating algae growth.
On July 3, 2026, the Department of the Interior was soliciting bids for a long-term maintenance contractor, including an annual purge of muck.
Congressional Democrats (Rep. Robert Garcia, House Oversight) demanded answers and investigations from contractors over the no-bid renovation.

data_points.metric: Final cost of Reflecting Pool renovation
Final cost of Reflecting Pool renovation (Scientific American figure)
No-bid nanobubbler cleaning system contract (Trump-donor company)
Reported arrests for alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
